# Git ブランチ戦略の設定駆動化と SoTOHE 本体 develop 採用

## Tasks (23/23 resolved)

### S1 — Core domain types and usecase port

> Introduce MergeMethod and BranchStrategySnapshot in libs/domain/, extend TrackMetadata with a required branch_strategy_snapshot field.
> Define BranchStrategyPort in libs/usecase/ as the single abstraction boundary for reading the effective branch strategy; add TrackService::switch_base.
> These two tasks provide the domain and usecase port foundation; switch_base's real branch-switch behavior is completed when T011 wiring overrides its temporary failure default.

Update TrackMetadata in libs/domain/src/track.rs: add branch_strategy_snapshot: BranchStrategySnapshot field, update constructors new() and with_branch() to accept branch_strategy_snapshot: BranchStrategySnapshot as a required argument, add branch_strategy_snapshot() accessor. Register branch_strategy module in libs/domain/src/lib.rs. Keep TrackBranch::try_new prefix invariant ('track/') unchanged (CN-04). (`534672676e42736eb46b9786339b93d54888abe6`)

### S3 — Replace git rev-parse main fallback (IN-04)

> Replace all runtime git rev-parse main fallback calls (used as diff-base when .commit_hash is absent) with the effective strategy's base_branch().
> Covers libs/infrastructure (gate_state, commit_hash_store), libs/infrastructure PR test fixtures (gh_cli.rs), and apps/cli-composition (dry/shared, review_v2/shared, fixpoint_resolve, dry.rs tests).
> Each sub-task is isolated to its review group to keep per-scope diffs reviewable.

- [x] **T006**: libs/infrastructure/ (fallback) — Update libs/infrastructure/src/track/gate_state.rs:293-306 and :849: change the git rev-parse main fallback to accept a base_branch: &str parameter and invoke git with argv-style arguments (e.g. command/program = "git", args = ["rev-parse", base_branch]) instead of constructing a shell command string such as `format!("git rev-parse {base_branch}")`. Treat configured branch names as external input at command boundaries: pass them as separate process arguments and never interpolate them into shell text. Update error messages to reference the configured branch name. Update libs/infrastructure/src/dry_check/commit_hash_store.rs:55 doc comment: replace 'main' with 'configured base branch'.

### S7 — ADR 1328 cli-composition layer-boundary completion: last relocated adapter, fixpoint-resolve driver split, and dry render migration (GO-03)

> Relocate the one remaining port-impl adapter left in apps/cli-composition (FsDryApprovalFactoryAdapter) into libs/infrastructure, closing out the D7 adapter-relocation sweep started by earlier gate-state and diff-base-resolver moves (IN-11/AC-16).
> Add a new usecase module (fixpoint_resolve_driver) providing the input/output DTOs, four fine-grained secondary ports (workspace-context resolution, dry-check config loading, dry-gate factory, gate-state factory), the primary ApplicationService, and an Interactor that performs the fixpoint-resolve orchestration itself by invoking those four ports plus the existing usecase::fixpoint_resolve::FixpointResolveService directly (ADR 1328 D4: orchestration belongs in usecase, not in an infrastructure SecondaryAdapter) — corrects the 2026-07-02 plan-artifacts final-review P1 finding against an earlier single-port pass-through draft of this module (IN-12/AC-17).
> Add a matching libs/infrastructure module providing four thin adapters, each implementing exactly one of the four fine-grained ports (ADR 1328 D7 / R1: SecondaryAdapter is infrastructure-only); move the invoke (interactor call) and render (format_fixpoint_step) responsibilities for fixpoint-resolve from TrackCompositionRoot into cli_driver::track::TrackDriver, and update TrackCompositionRoot to construct and inject the four infrastructure adapters plus the usecase Interactor into TrackDriver without invoking either itself (ADR 1328 D2); apps/cli dispatches through TrackDriver::handle instead of calling the composition root directly (IN-12/AC-17).
> Change DryDriverPort/DryDriverService's dry_write and dry_check_approved methods to return structured pre-render DTOs (DryWriteOutcome, DryCheckApprovedOutcome) instead of a pre-formatted DryDriverOutcome, and move the corresponding text rendering from apps/cli-composition (dry_write_outcome / dry_check_approved_outcome) into apps/cli-driver's DryDriver (IN-13/AC-18).
> S7 execution order is encoded by this section's task_ids list and task text: T016 and T017 must land before T018; T018 before T019; T019 before T020; T021 before T022 and T023, with T022/T023 independent after T021.
> CN-07/AC-19's byte-for-byte preservation claim applies to relocated invoke/render paths and their existing CLI output text, exit codes, and test coverage. T018's fail-closed metadata/config behavior is governed separately by IN-06/IN-07/CN-03/CN-06 and must be tested as an explicit error path rather than described as a no-op relocation.

- [x] **T016**: libs/infrastructure/ + apps/cli-composition/ (IN-11 relocation) — Relocate the private `FsDryApprovalFactoryAdapter` struct and its `DryApprovalFactoryPort` impl (currently defined at apps/cli-composition/src/track/fixpoint_resolve.rs:64-91) into a new module libs/infrastructure/src/dry_check/approval_factory.rs (module_path `dry_check::approval_factory`), mirroring the sibling `FsDiffBaseResolverAdapter` relocation already done in libs/infrastructure/src/dry_check/diff_base_resolver.rs. Make the struct and its `build_approval` method pub. Register the module in libs/infrastructure/src/dry_check/mod.rs (`pub mod approval_factory;` plus `pub use approval_factory::FsDryApprovalFactoryAdapter;`, alongside the existing `diff_base_resolver` entries). In apps/cli-composition/src/track/fixpoint_resolve.rs, delete the local struct+impl block and add a re-export shim import from `infrastructure::dry_check::approval_factory` (matching the comment style already used for the `NoOpDryApprovalService` and `FsDiffBaseResolverAdapter` re-export shims in the same file); `make_dry_gate_interactor` continues to construct `Arc::new(FsDryApprovalFactoryAdapter)` unchanged. No behavior change (CN-07/AC-19): confirm `cargo make test` passes. IN-11/AC-16.
- [x] **T017**: libs/usecase/ (rewritten per the 2026-07-02 plan-artifacts final-review P1 finding: ADR 1328 D4 requires application orchestration to live inside a usecase `ApplicationService`, not delegated wholesale to a single-port pass-through `Interactor` backed by one infrastructure `SecondaryAdapter`) — Add a new module libs/usecase/src/fixpoint_resolve_driver.rs (mirroring `FixpointDryGateInteractor`'s multi-fine-grained-port-factory shape in fixpoint_resolve.rs, not `DryDriverInteractor`'s single-port pass-through shape) defining: `FixpointResolveDriverInput` struct (Dto; fields track_id: String, current_branch: String, items_dir: std::path::PathBuf; Debug + Clone); `FixpointResolveDriverOutcome` enum (Dto; variants RunDfp, RunRfp { scopes: Vec<String> }, RunRefVerify, Commit, Failure { message: String }; Debug + Clone); `FixpointWorkspaceContext` struct (Dto; fields repo_root: PathBuf, canonical_items_dir: PathBuf, canonical_root: PathBuf, base_branch: String; Debug + Clone); `FixpointWorkspaceContextError` enum (ErrorType; single variant Unavailable(String); Debug + Clone + Display + Error); `DryCheckConfigLoaderError` enum (ErrorType; single variant Unavailable(String); Debug + Clone + Display + Error); `FixpointWorkspaceContextPort` trait (SecondaryPort; Send + Sync; method resolve_context(&self, items_dir: &std::path::Path, track_id: &domain::TrackId) -> Result<FixpointWorkspaceContext, FixpointWorkspaceContextError>); `DryCheckConfigLoaderPort` trait (SecondaryPort; Send + Sync; method load(&self, repo_root: &std::path::Path) -> Result<(usecase::dry_check::DryCheckConfig, domain::dry_check::DryCheckConfigFingerprint), DryCheckConfigLoaderError>); `FixpointDryGateFactoryPort` trait (SecondaryPort; Send + Sync; method build(&self, base_branch: &str) -> Arc<dyn FixpointDryGateService>); `FixpointGateStateFactoryPort` trait (SecondaryPort; Send + Sync; methods build_review_gate(&self, items_dir: &std::path::Path, base_branch: &str) -> Arc<dyn ReviewGateStatePort> and build_ref_verify_gate(&self, items_dir: &std::path::Path) -> Arc<dyn RefVerifyGateStatePort>); `FixpointResolveDriverService` trait (ApplicationService; Send + Sync; single method fixpoint_resolve(&self, input: FixpointResolveDriverInput) -> FixpointResolveDriverOutcome); `FixpointResolveDriverInteractor` struct (Interactor; holds the four ports above as private Arc<dyn Port> fields; constructor new(workspace_context: Arc<dyn FixpointWorkspaceContextPort>, dry_config_loader: Arc<dyn DryCheckConfigLoaderPort>, dry_gate_factory: Arc<dyn FixpointDryGateFactoryPort>, gate_state_factory: Arc<dyn FixpointGateStateFactoryPort>) -> Self).

`FixpointResolveDriverInteractor` implements `FixpointResolveDriverService` by performing the entire fixpoint-resolve orchestration itself (ADR 1328 D4 — no infrastructure adapter sits between this interactor and the domain-level resolve call): (1) validate track_id via `domain::TrackId::try_new` and current_branch via `usecase::fixpoint_resolve::FixpointCurrentBranch::try_new`, mapping either validation failure straight to `Outcome::Failure { message: e.to_string() }` (this replaces the cli_composition-private `validate_track_id_str` call the removed method used — infrastructure/usecase cannot depend on that helper, and `TrackId::try_new` alone reproduces the identical check); (2) enforce the TrackNotActive branch-mismatch guard as pure logic (`expected_branch = format!("track/{}", track_id.as_ref())`), mapping a mismatch to `Outcome::Failure { message: FixpointResolveError::TrackNotActive { branch: expected_branch }.to_string() }`; (3) call `workspace_context.resolve_context(&input.items_dir, &track_id)` to obtain a `FixpointWorkspaceContext`, mapping `Err` to `Outcome::Failure`; (4) call `dry_config_loader.load(&context.repo_root)` to obtain `(dry_config, current_config_fingerprint)`, mapping `Err` to `Outcome::Failure`; (5) call `dry_gate_factory.build(&context.base_branch)` to obtain a `FixpointDryGateService`, then invoke its `resolve_dry_gate` with a `FixpointDryGateCommand` built from `track_dir = context.canonical_items_dir.join(track_id.as_ref())`, `canonical_root`, `repo_root`, `dry_config.clone()`, `current_config_fingerprint`; the interactor must not mutate the process CWD or otherwise manage process-global execution context, and any git working-directory compatibility needed for dry-gate discovery must stay behind the injected infrastructure adapters/command runners using the explicit `repo_root` carried in the command (or fail through their port errors) — mapping `Err` to `Outcome::Failure { message: e.to_string() }`; (6) call `gate_state_factory.build_review_gate(&context.canonical_items_dir, &context.base_branch)` and `.build_ref_verify_gate(&context.canonical_items_dir)`; (7) construct `usecase::fixpoint_resolve::FixpointResolveInteractor::new(dry_config, dry_gate_output.dry_approval, review_gate, ref_verify_gate)` and invoke `FixpointResolveService::resolve(&FixpointResolveCommand { track_id, current_branch, current_fragment_refs: dry_gate_output.current_fragment_refs })` directly; (8) map `Ok(FixpointStep::RunDfp/RunRfp{scopes}/RunRefVerify/Commit)` to the matching `Outcome` variant (`RunRfp`'s `scopes: Vec<String>` must be built via `scopes.as_set().iter().cloned().collect()` so the sorted BTreeSet iteration order is preserved verbatim, needed by T019's render arm for CN-07/AC-19), and `Err(FixpointResolveError)` to `Outcome::Failure { message: e.to_string() }`.

Register the module in libs/usecase/src/lib.rs (`pub mod fixpoint_resolve_driver;`, alongside the existing `pub mod fixpoint_resolve;` and `pub mod dry_driver;` entries). This module does not modify the existing usecase::fixpoint_resolve module or any of its ports/services — it only consumes them.

Add unit tests using stub ports (mirroring `FixpointDryGateInteractor`'s existing stub-test style in fixpoint_resolve.rs) covering: each of the four `FixpointStep` outcomes mapped to its `Outcome` variant, and a `FixpointWorkspaceContextPort` failure, a `DryCheckConfigLoaderPort` failure, and a failing `FixpointDryGateService::resolve_dry_gate` returned by the built `FixpointDryGateFactoryPort` service, each mapped to `Outcome::Failure`. Also relocate three of the six wiring-path unit tests currently in apps/cli-composition/src/track/fixpoint_resolve.rs — test_fixpoint_resolve_wrong_branch_returns_track_not_active_error, test_fixpoint_resolve_empty_current_branch_returns_invalid_current_branch_error, and test_fixpoint_resolve_invalid_track_id_returns_error (the three that short-circuit before any port is called) — into this module's own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`, adapted to call `FixpointResolveDriverInteractor::fixpoint_resolve` (via `FixpointResolveDriverService`, with stub ports) directly with a `FixpointResolveDriverInput`, asserting `FixpointResolveDriverOutcome::Failure { message }` carries the equivalent diagnostic content as each test's original assertion (adapt the exact substring checked to the new message text — CN-07/AC-19's CLI-output-text-preservation guarantee is enforced at the apps/cli dispatch boundary by T020's relocated dispatch-level tests, which only assert on the `CliError` variant, not on specific wording; it is not enforced by this internal usecase-level test's own substring choice). The other three wiring-path tests (items_dir outside repo / missing coverage record / items_dir is a file) need real adapters and are relocated to T018 instead. This module is purely additive to the rest of the workspace — nothing outside it references these new types yet, so it compiles standalone. IN-12/AC-17.
- [x] **T018**: Ordered after T016 and T017 by the S7 task_ids sequence: this task consumes the T016-relocated approval factory and the T017 usecase ports. libs/infrastructure/ (rewritten per the same 2026-07-02 plan-artifacts final-review P1 finding as T017: ADR 1328 D7 / the R1 role-layer matrix require the `SecondaryAdapter` role to live in `libs/infrastructure` only, and each such adapter must perform a single narrow I/O concern rather than orchestrate the whole fixpoint-resolve flow) — Create a new module libs/infrastructure/src/track/fixpoint_resolve_driver.rs (module_path `track::fixpoint_resolve_driver`, mirroring the `FsDryApprovalFactoryAdapter` relocation pattern from T016/IN-11: zero-field unit structs, constructed by name, no constructor fn) defining four adapters, each implementing exactly one T017 port: (1) `FsFixpointWorkspaceContextAdapter` implementing `FixpointWorkspaceContextPort::resolve_context` — reproduces the CWD-anchored repo discovery via `infrastructure::git_cli::SystemGitRepo::discover()` -> repo_root (canonicalized); items_dir canonicalization (absolute path canonicalized directly, relative path anchored to repo_root first) and containment check against repo_root; the is_dir() check; derivation of canonical_root (the `<project-root>/track/items` -> `<project-root>` parent-walk, reimplemented locally since libs/infrastructure cannot depend on the cli_composition-private `resolve_project_root` helper) canonicalized; and the base_branch read from `<canonical_items_dir>/<track_id>/metadata.json`'s `branch_strategy_snapshot.base_branch` via `infrastructure::track::codec::decode`, with missing metadata.json and any other I/O/decode error mapped to `FixpointWorkspaceContextError::Unavailable` (fail-closed; no branch-name fallback) — this preserves the fail-closed branch-strategy invariant from IN-06/IN-07/CN-03/CN-06; its visible diagnostic is covered by this task's error-path tests and is not part of CN-07's byte-for-byte relocation guarantee. (2) `FsDryCheckConfigLoaderAdapter` implementing `DryCheckConfigLoaderPort::load` — calls `infrastructure::dry_check::DryCheckConfig::load(&repo_root.join(".harness/config/dry-check.json"))`, then lifts the result into the usecase-level `dry_check::DryCheckConfig` via the same field-by-field conversion already implemented by `cli_composition::dry::dry_checker_config::build_usecase_dry_check_config` (known_bad_injection_rate_percent / known_bad_detection_threshold_percent -> `DryCheckPercent::try_new`, max_parallelism -> `DryCheckParallelism::try_new`, enabled passed through) — reimplemented locally in this adapter since libs/infrastructure cannot depend on apps/cli-composition — and returns `(usecase_config, infra_config.fingerprint())`; maps any load or validation error to `DryCheckConfigLoaderError::Unavailable`. (3) `FsFixpointDryGateFactoryAdapter` implementing `FixpointDryGateFactoryPort::build(base_branch)` — reproduces unchanged the wiring previously done by the removed `TrackCompositionRoot::make_dry_gate_interactor` helper: constructs `usecase::fixpoint_resolve::FixpointDryGateInteractor` from `infrastructure::dry_check::noop_approval::NoOpDryApprovalService`, `infrastructure::dry_check::diff_base_resolver::FsDiffBaseResolverAdapter::new(base_branch.to_owned())`, `infrastructure::dry_check::FsDryCorpusMetaAdapter`, a `usecase::dry_check::DryFragmentPipelineInteractor` wired with `infrastructure::dry_check::GitDryCheckDiffGetter` + `infrastructure::semantic_dup::CodeFragmentExtractorAdapter`, and `infrastructure::dry_check::approval_factory::FsDryApprovalFactoryAdapter` (the T016-relocated in-crate adapter, referenced directly instead of via the cli_composition re-export shim) — returns it as `Arc<dyn usecase::fixpoint_resolve::FixpointDryGateService>`. (4) `FsFixpointGateStateFactoryAdapter` implementing `FixpointGateStateFactoryPort` — `build_review_gate` constructs `infrastructure::track::gate_state::FsReviewGateStateAdapter::new(items_dir.to_path_buf(), base_branch.to_owned())`; `build_ref_verify_gate` constructs `infrastructure::track::gate_state::FsRefVerifyGateStateAdapter::new(items_dir.to_path_buf())` — both previously constructed inline in the removed `TrackCompositionRoot::fixpoint_resolve`.

Register the module in libs/infrastructure/src/track/mod.rs (`pub mod fixpoint_resolve_driver;`).

Relocate three of the six wiring-path unit tests currently in apps/cli-composition/src/track/fixpoint_resolve.rs — test_fixpoint_resolve_items_dir_outside_repo_returns_error, test_fixpoint_resolve_missing_coverage_record_with_enabled_true_returns_run_dfp, and test_fixpoint_resolve_items_dir_is_file_returns_error (the three that need real git repos / real filesystem / real dry-gate wiring) — into this module's own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`, adapted to construct `usecase::fixpoint_resolve_driver::FixpointResolveDriverInteractor::new` wired with all four real adapters defined in this module (`Arc::new(FsFixpointWorkspaceContextAdapter)`, `Arc::new(FsDryCheckConfigLoaderAdapter)`, `Arc::new(FsFixpointDryGateFactoryAdapter)`, `Arc::new(FsFixpointGateStateFactoryAdapter)`) and calling `FixpointResolveDriverService::fixpoint_resolve` with a `FixpointResolveDriverInput` built from each test's existing fixture (temp git repos, .commit_hash, metadata.json, .harness/config/dry-check.json), asserting on the returned `FixpointResolveDriverOutcome` variant (`RunDfp` / `Failure { message }`) instead of `CommandOutcome` / `CompositionError`, preserving each test's existing assertions on the resulting text/error content where that legacy behavior is intentionally preserved (CN-07/AC-19), and asserting the explicit fail-closed diagnostic where branch-strategy invariants apply. This module is purely additive — it implements the four T017 ports, but nothing outside this module references these adapters yet, so it compiles standalone without touching cli_driver, cli_composition, or apps/cli. IN-12/AC-17.
- [x] **T019**: Ordered after T018 by the S7 task_ids sequence: this task wires the usecase driver service and infrastructure adapters added by T017/T018. apps/cli-driver/ + apps/cli-composition/ (wiring flip; corrects the same 2026-07-02 P1 finding) — In apps/cli-driver/src/track.rs: add a `FixpointResolve { track_id: String, current_branch: String, items_dir: std::path::PathBuf }` variant to `TrackInput` (field-for-field mirror of `usecase::fixpoint_resolve_driver::FixpointResolveDriverInput`). Add a `fixpoint_resolve_service: Arc<dyn usecase::fixpoint_resolve_driver::FixpointResolveDriverService>` field to `TrackDriver`; change `TrackDriver::new` to take it as a second parameter. Add a `TrackInput::FixpointResolve` dispatch arm in `TrackDriver::handle` that calls `self.fixpoint_resolve_service.fixpoint_resolve(FixpointResolveDriverInput { track_id, current_branch, items_dir })` and renders the resulting `FixpointResolveDriverOutcome` into a `CommandOutcome` using the exact text contract previously produced by `cli_composition::track::fixpoint_resolve::format_fixpoint_step` (RunDfp -> "run-dfp"; RunRfp { scopes } -> "run-rfp scopes=<s1>,<s2>..." via `scopes.join(",")` — scopes already arrives in sorted order per T017's BTreeSet-preserving conversion, so no re-sort is needed here; RunRefVerify -> "run-ref-verify"; Commit -> "commit"; all via `CommandOutcome::success`), plus a `Failure { message }` arm producing `CommandOutcome::failure(Some(message))` (mirroring how the removed `TrackCompositionRoot::fixpoint_resolve` surfaced errors). Relocate the `format_fixpoint_step` output-shape unit tests (test_format_fixpoint_step_run_dfp / _run_rfp_single_scope / _run_rfp_multiple_scopes_in_btreeset_order / _run_ref_verify / _commit, currently in apps/cli-composition/src/track/fixpoint_resolve.rs:400-437) into apps/cli-driver/src/track.rs, adapted to exercise the new render logic directly with `FixpointResolveDriverOutcome` inputs (e.g. `RunRfp { scopes: vec!["code".to_owned(), "plan-artifacts".to_owned()] }` in place of a domain `ReviewScopeSet`) instead of `FixpointStep` (cli_driver may only depend on usecase, not domain) — CN-07/AC-19: identical output strings.

In apps/cli-composition/src/track/composition_root.rs: update `TrackCompositionRoot::track_driver()` to also construct `usecase::fixpoint_resolve_driver::FixpointResolveDriverInteractor::new(Arc::new(infrastructure::track::fixpoint_resolve_driver::FsFixpointWorkspaceContextAdapter), Arc::new(infrastructure::track::fixpoint_resolve_driver::FsDryCheckConfigLoaderAdapter), Arc::new(infrastructure::track::fixpoint_resolve_driver::FsFixpointDryGateFactoryAdapter), Arc::new(infrastructure::track::fixpoint_resolve_driver::FsFixpointGateStateFactoryAdapter))`, wrap it in `Arc::new(...)`, and pass it as the second argument to `cli_driver::track::TrackDriver::new(service, fixpoint_resolve_service)`. `track_driver()` only constructs and injects this adapter chain — it never calls `FixpointResolveDriverService::fixpoint_resolve` itself (ADR 1328 D2: composition root is wire-only).

Deliberately leave apps/cli-composition/src/track/fixpoint_resolve.rs (the `FixpointResolveInput` DTO, the `TrackCompositionRoot::fixpoint_resolve` inherent method, the `make_dry_gate_interactor` helper, `format_fixpoint_step`, and their existing tests) untouched in this task: `apps/cli` still calls that method directly and has not yet been switched to the new `TrackDriver`-based path (that switch is T020), so the old method must keep compiling and working exactly as before. This produces a transitional state where the old direct-call path and the new `TrackDriver`-based path exist side by side and both compile — the same staged-compatibility technique already used for the T016 re-export shim (CN-07: staged commits preserving call-compatibility during the migration). `composition_root.rs` is the only call site of `TrackDriver::new` in the workspace, so updating it in this same task keeps `cli_composition` compiling once `TrackDriver::new`'s signature changes; `apps/cli` requires no change in this task because the old `TrackCompositionRoot::fixpoint_resolve` entrypoint it calls is untouched. IN-12/AC-17.
